response_text:
As of my last update in October 2023, there is no official information suggesting that the Rust programming language has added an optional garbage collector in the upcoming 2024 edition. Rust is well-known for its ownership model and lifetime management, which provide memory safety without the need for a garbage collector. This is one of its core features and selling points.

If there have been any announcements or developments regarding a garbage collector in Rust since my last update, I would recommend checking the official Rust blog, the Rust Programming Language Forum, or the Rust GitHub repository for the most current information. These sources will provide accurate and up-to-date details on any new features or changes in Rust.

If a garbage collector were to be introduced as an optional feature, these official channels would also provide documentation on how to enable and use it. For now, Rust continues to rely on its ownership and borrowing system for memory management.
